title: New Evangelion album OutTakes from Evangelion
display_title: New Evangelion album OutTakes from Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Thu Mar 10, 2016 1:27 pm I was browsing the internet a while ago, and I somehow stumbled upon a forum discussing this page on HMV Japan: http://www.hmv.co.jp/artist_%E9%B7%BA%E ... on_6882838 If this page is correct (and I think it is, since looking up its tag of KICA-3262 brings me to pages of the same album), a new Evangelion album containing outtakes from the show's soundtrack (which I imagine must be unused songs or variations, similar to the S2 Works release back in 1998) is going to come out on the 30th of June. It might be based on the Rebuild of Evangelion movies, since the title only has the Evangelion name used in the official release, so we could very well hear a few new songs originally intended for the three movies. Discuss the album, and anything interesting that comes out of it here. UPDATE: Since nothing appeared on websites such as Amazon and the like, I've re-checked those sites and discovered that the release date was a misprint. It's actually the 30th of June it looks to be coming out on, not the 30th of March. Massive apologies for the misinformation. Carry on.

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Thu Mar 10, 2016 3:13 pm Interesting! I'm not seeing it anywhere else but I'll definitely have to look into buying it when it comes up on CDJapan or elsewhere.

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Mar 10, 2016 6:54 pm Brace yourself for 100 variations of Jikai Yokoku,

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by Dima » Fri Mar 11, 2016 7:54 am ^ I am okay with this.

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Sun May 08, 2016 7:37 pm NEW UPDATE ON THE ALBUM: On Shiro's official website, Ro-Jam, it was confirmed that the OutTakes album will be released on the 29th of July, the same day his soundtrack to Anno's Godzilla comes out. Some extra information was also given out on Yahoo! Japan, which is as follows: (in Japanese, anyway) SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Mon Jun 20, 2016 6:47 pm Evangelion.co.jp updated with more information about the album, including cover art and the track listing. Track listing  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Wed Jun 22, 2016 5:26 am I recognized some of the listed numbers as being part of the S2 Works album (basically every song Sagisu every composed for the original series and films, including alternate takes and unused songs), so I had a look at the tracklist and have gotten some ideas. Track 1. M11 is the unused EoE song Everything You've Ever Dreamed , so this is likely going to take the original vocals for that song and put new music to it (similar to the piano version that appeared in the PianoForté album). Tracks 2 and 3. M3 is for The Passage of Emptiness , the song that plays at the end of the MP-Eva fight in the EoE. Considering that the next track contains the word emptiness in it, I can only assume that the song will be covered differently per track. (The Jazz Night album did this as well) Track 4. London-Derry seems a bit odd, but then I remembered the credits of until You come to me, which listed its song Danny Boy as being from County Derry. So it must be the beautiful orchestral version of Danny Boy that played for most of that short. Track 5. M5 is an unused, untitled song intended for EoE at some point. A grim orchestral piece some have compared to Mozart's Lacrimosa. Track 18. A4 is the codename for Borderline Case , from the original series. (That's why the codename appeared in the name of Quelconque 56 avec A4 ; it means something along the lines of Piano Song 56 WITH A4.) I don't recognize the rest from anywhere, so that's all I've got to offer on the subject until the album comes out. Come on, July 29th!

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jun 22, 2016 6:03 am FrDougal9000 wrote: Track 4. London-Derry seems a bit odd, but then I remembered the credits of until You come to me, which listed its song Danny Boy as being from County Derry. So it must be the beautiful orchestral version of Danny Boy that played for most of that short.

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by cyharding » Tue Aug 02, 2016 11:16 pm I'm wondering if anyone recieved their copy as I got mine today, though I haven't listened to it yet.

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Wed Aug 03, 2016 2:08 am cyharding wrote: I'm wondering if anyone recieved their copy as I got mine today, though I haven't listened to it yet.

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by cyharding » Wed Aug 03, 2016 10:18 pm I see. Does anyone have any questions?

[]: Re: New Evangelion album: OutTakes from "Evangelion"             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Aug 04, 2016 2:48 am I expect mine within a couple of days.
